ontology.foodon_gap_note: No exact FoodOn entry for Tau Kwa Pau; mapped to 'stuffed food product' as the closest defensible parent class. Tau Kwa Pau is a Singaporean Chinese dish of fried beancurd stuffed with pork, fishcake, yam, egg and cucumber.
ontology.foodex2_description: Soybean curd (tofu) based dish — approximate match; Tau Kwa Pau is a composite stuffed fried beancurd dish with mixed fillings.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a specific code for Tau Kwa Pau. A0CKM (soybean curd products) used as the closest defensible anchor. Composite dish nature acknowledged.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for Tau Kwa Pau or a sufficiently close composite soy-based dish.

serving.natural_language_portion: One typical serving of Tau Kwa Pau is approximately one whole piece weighing around 150 g, commonly consumed as a snack or light meal at Singaporean hawker centres.

nutrient_source.title: Tau Kwa Pau — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Composition Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Tau Kwa Pau (CR ID: F09050016). All core macros and secondary nutrients are taken from this canonical entry.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson et al.; analogous estimate based on mixed soy-pork stuffed bun with moderate carbohydrate content
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Tau Kwa Pau in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI estimated at 52 (low) based on the composite profile: fried beancurd (tofu, low GI ~15), mixed protein fillings (pork, fishcake, egg — negligible GI contribution), and a small bun/wrapper component (moderate GI ~55–65). The protein-dominant, moderate-carbohydrate profile (7.8 g/100 g) supports a low overall GI. Glycemic load per 150 g serving = (52 × 7.8 × 1.5) / 100 = 6.1 g, classified as low GL. Estimation basis documented per Veritas GI policy.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 626 mg per 100 g — exceeds the 600 mg/100 g high-concern threshold
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit Tau Kwa Pau to half a piece per occasion, request reduced soy sauce or sauce on the side, and account for this dish within a daily sodium target of under 2000 mg to avoid exacerbating blood pressure elevation.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low carbohydrate content (7.8 g/100 g), high protein (10.5 g/100 g), and estimated low GI (~52) support stable postprandial glucose
type2_diabetes.guidance: Tau Kwa Pau is suitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes when consumed as one piece; the high protein content from tofu, pork and fishcake slows gastric emptying and blunts postprandial glucose rise, though the elevated sodium warrants attention in those with concurrent hypertension.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High sodium (626 mg/100 g) and moderate fat (9.2 g/100 g) with saturated fat contribution from streaky pork and deep-frying
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should consume Tau Kwa Pau sparingly due to its high sodium content and saturated fat from streaky pork and deep-fried beancurd; if consumed, limit to half a piece and pair with unseasoned vegetables to reduce overall sodium and saturated fat load.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Sodium at 626 mg/100 g exceeds the 400 mg/100 g RA threshold, promoting pro-inflammatory fluid retention
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid or strictly limit Tau Kwa Pau due to its high sodium content, which can worsen RA-related tissue swelling and inflammation; request minimal soy sauce and limit to a small portion if consumed.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Tau Kwa Pau (豆干包) is a popular Singaporean Chinese hawker snack consisting of a deep-fried firm beancurd (tau kwa) that is sliced open and stuffed with a savoury combination of sliced streaky pork, fishcake, fried yam, chopped egg, and fresh cucumber, typically dressed with sweet soy sauce or chilli sauce. The dish is served at room temperature or slightly warm and is a common street food found at hawker centres and food courts across Singapore. Texturally, the exterior of the beancurd is crisp from deep-frying while the interior remains soft and absorbent, soaking up the sauce. The filling provides a contrast of textures — tender pork, springy fishcake, starchy yam, and crunchy cucumber. Nutritionally, per 100 g, Tau Kwa Pau provides 156 kcal, 10.5 g protein, 9.2 g fat, and 7.8 g carbohydrate, with a notably high sodium content of 626 mg primarily from soy sauce seasoning. The high protein and low carbohydrate profile gives it a low estimated glycaemic index (~52), making it relatively suitable for blood glucose management, though the high sodium makes it a concern for hypertension and cardiovascular disease. The dish is not halal due to the pork content and is not suitable for vegetarians or vegans. It is classified as a processed, composite soy-based dish in the Singaporean food group of meat and alternatives.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (CR ID: F09050016), which is the canonical primary source for Singaporean dishes. All core macros and secondary nutrients are exact matches from this approved source. GI is an analogous estimate documented per policy.
notes: sugar_g is 0.0 — confirmed zero per HPB Singapore Foodo database; the dish contains no added sugar and the savoury filling components contribute negligible free sugars. Saturated fat estimated at 2.8 g/100 g based on the composite profile of streaky pork (dominant saturated fat contributor) and deep-fried beancurd; HPB does not report saturated fat breakdown separately for this entry, so this value is an approximation from USDA SR Legacy data for analogous components. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid profile, micronutrient panel, and moisture data for Tau Kwa Pau are not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for this composite dish as of 2026. The dietary_tags in the input incorrectly listed 'vegetarian' and 'plant_based'; these have been corrected to false as the dish contains streaky pork and fishcake. The input condition key 'diabetes' has been updated to 'type2_diabetes' per Veritas naming rules.
